file_name: GWG HOLDINGS, INC. - ORDERLY MARKETING AGREEMENT.txt

perturbation.original_text: 1.1 The shares of Stock held by each Seller Trust and subject to this OMA shall be as set out on Schedule A hereto, which Schedule may be amended from time to time in writing by the Parties in accordance with Section 4.1 below.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.1 The shares of Stock held by each Seller Trust and subject to this OMA shall be as set out on Schedule A hereto, which Schedule may be modified from time to time by the Lead Investor in accordance with Section 4.1 below.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Parties' to 'Lead Investor' introduces ambiguity and contradiction. Section 4.1 allows 'Parties' to amend the agreement, but Section 1.1 now implies that only the 'Lead Investor' can amend Schedule A. This creates confusion as to who has the authority to change the schedule, impacting the agreement's enforceability. It's not clear if this modification by the 'Lead Investor' requires other parties' consent, conflicting with the original intent of mutual agreement, and creates an In-Text Contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4.2 Amendments and Waivers. This OMA may be amended or modified in whole or in part, only by duly authorized agreement in writing executed by each of the Parties.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that the Parties could amend Schedule A, aligning with Section 4.2, which requires agreement by all Parties for amendments. The changed text grants the Lead Investor sole authority to modify Schedule A, directly contradicting Section 4.2, which requires agreement by all Parties for any amendment. This introduces a clear in-text contradiction concerning who has the authority to amend the agreement, making enforcement unclear and fitting the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: 1.4 The Seller Trusts and GWG intend to retain one or more nationally recognized bulge bracket investment banks (the "Bank") for the orderly marketing and resale of Shares pursuant to a separate engagement letter (the "Engagement Letter") to advise them in connection with the sale of the Tranches (together, the "Offerings").
perturbation.changed_text: 1.4 The Seller Trusts and GWG intend to retain one or more nationally recognized bulge bracket investment banks (the "Financial Institution") for the orderly marketing and resale of Shares pursuant to a separate engagement letter (the "Advisory Contract") to advise them in connection with the sale of the Tranches (together, the "Sales").
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'Bank' to 'Financial Institution', 'Engagement Letter' to 'Advisory Contract', and 'Offerings' to 'Sales,' the document creates ambiguity. Subsequent sections still refer to the original terms ('Bank', 'Engagement Letter', 'Offerings'), leading to confusion and potential disputes over which set of terms applies. This creates an In-Text Contradiction, specifically making it ambiguous when reffering to 'engagement Letter' in the next section.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1.5 After the Parties have agreed on the strategy for the marketing, sale and distribution of a Tranche, the Seller Trusts shall offer the Bank the right to serve as the lead left joint-book-running manager in connection with a best efforts distribution. GWG shall be entitled to appoint, in its discretion, an additional bank as joint book-running manager to participate in the distribution. The Seller Trusts further agree that in the event the Bank accepts such role it will be paid customary fees for the performance of its services in connection with such transactions and that such engagement will involve the execution of a standard form agreement with respect to the distribution of each Tranche, which may be in the form of a placement agency agreement, underwriting agreement or other appropriate agreement (each, a "Distribution Agreement"); provided, however, that nothing contained in this OMA or Engagement Letter shall require the Bank to underwrite or purchase all or any portion of a Tranche of Stock for its own account. Notwithstanding the foregoing, it is understood and agreed that the Bank or its affiliates may, solely at its discretion and without any obligation to do so, purchase Stock in any Tranche as principal.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Bank' to 'Financial Institution', 'Engagement Letter' to 'Advisory Contract', and 'Offerings' to 'Sales' introduces ambiguity. Section 1.5 refers to the 'Bank' and 'Engagement Letter'. Because these terms were changed, it is unclear whether these new terms or old terms are being referred to in this section. This creates an in-text contradiction, specifically a Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 4.1 Term; Termination; Withdrawal of Bank. (a) This OMA shall expire upon the earlier of (i) the first anniversary of the Effective Date and (ii) the date that all Shares of Stock of the Seller Trusts as set forth on Schedule A hereto have been sold (the "Term").
perturbation.changed_text: 4.1 Term; Termination; Withdrawal of Financial Advisor. (a) This OMA shall expire upon the earlier of (i) the first anniversary of the Effective Date and (ii) the date that all Shares of Stock of the Seller Trusts as set forth on Schedule A hereto have been sold (the "Duration").
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Bank' to 'Financial Advisor' and 'Term' to 'Duration' introduces inconsistency. Throughout the document, 'Bank' and 'Term' are consistently used, so introducing a new, different terminology creates confusion and ambiguity. This could lead to uncertainty in the agreement's interpretation and enforcement. This is an In-Text Contradiction because the definition of 'Term' is being changed and the rest of the document refers to the original definition.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Notwithstanding the foregoing, this OMA may be terminated with or without cause at any time after the Effective Date and without liability or continuing obligation by any of the Parties hereto (i) by mutual written agreement of all of the Parties; and (ii) in writing by the Trust Advisors in their sole discretion. - 4 - (b) The Engagement Letter shall provide that the Bank may terminate its engagement at any time upon not less than 45 days' prior written notice to the other Parties. In the event the Bank provides such notice at any time prior to the sale of greater than 50% of the Shares and within 12 months of the Effective Date, then GWG and the Trust Advisors may elect (A) to terminate this OMA or (B) to continue this OMA and to seek to appoint a substitute investment bank, in which case GWG, after consultation with the Trust Advisors, shall be entitled to appoint a substitute investment bank to serve as the lead joint-book-running manager for the sale of the Shares. In the event the Bank terminates the Engagement Letter at any time following the sale of greater than 50% of the Shares and within 12 months of the Effective Date, the termination thereof shall constitute a concurrent Termination of this OMA.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Bank' to 'Financial Advisor' and 'Term' to 'Duration' creates a contradiction because the contradicted text refers specifically to 'Bank' and implicitly to 'Term' as the defined period of the OMA. The original text explicitly defined the expiration using 'Term', and the contradicted text refers to actions and consequences within that 'Term'. By changing 'Term' to 'Duration', the agreement loses a consistent, defined period, causing ambiguity regarding the validity and enforcement of those actions. This misalignment introduces uncertainty regarding which financial entity has the right to terminate based on the 'Engagement Letter', and introduces uncertainty as to which defined time period applies to this agreement as it relates to termination, violating Misaligned Terminology, thus leading to In-Text Contradiction.
